channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ananse trains African entrepreneurs to boost fashion industry
item.description: The article highlights how Ananse Centre for Design's Fashion Incubator Scholarship Programme is empowering over 7,000 creatives across Nigeria, Ghana, Kenya, Senegal, Côte d’Ivoire, and South Africa....

item.title: Public Protector Report Highlights Systemic Failures in Gauteng's Food Safety System
item.description: The Public Protector's report reveals that Gauteng's food safety system is fundamentally compromised due to systemic governance failures, corruption, and weak enforcement. Despite measures taken after...

item.title: First Nations Chiefs Urge Federal and Provincial Governments to Protect Treaty Rights Amid Alberta Separatism
item.description: First Nations chiefs from across Canada have passed a resolution demanding the federal and provincial governments protect treaty rights against threats posed by Alberta's separatism. The Assembly of F...

item.title: Bauchi State Governorship Race 2027: Key Candidates and Political Dynamics
item.description: The article analyzes the 2027 governorship race in Bauchi State, highlighting five key candidates from different political parties. It discusses their backgrounds, including former governors, senators...

item.title: Global Shifts as Trump's Dollar Tactics Prompt Exploration of New Currencies
item.description: The article examines how the United States' monetary policies under Donald Trump are reshaping global financial dynamics. While the US dollar remains dominant, nations like China are developing digita...

item.title: Kenyan Man Freed After Imprisonment in Zimbabwe During South Africa Trek
item.description: Peter Githami, 26, a Kenyan man, was arrested at the Beitbridge border while attempting to walk from South Africa to Kenya. His mother, Mary, had already returned to Kenya alone after surviving xenoph...

item.title: Justin Bieber enjoys golf and cigar before FIFA World Cup halftime performance
item.description: Justin Bieber has been spotted smoking a cigar while playing golf in The Hamptons, days before his scheduled performance at the FIFA World Cup halftime show. The 32-year-old pop star, who is set to pe...

item.title: San Francisco Bay Boat Capsize: Search Continues for Missing Bodies and Wreckage Recovery
item.description: The sunken remains of the Volare, a 49-foot cabin cruiser, have been located at the bottom of San Francisco Bay near Alcatraz Island following a tragic incident that killed four of its 20 passengers. ...

item.title: Tanzania Receives Praise for SDG Progress at UN Forum

item.title: A Bath Full of Marbles in the Death Valley: A Humorous Geographical Joke
item.description: The article recounts the story of Wendell Moyer, a chemist turned hiker, who created a humorous geographical joke in Death Valley, California. Due to a mapping error, the 'Marble Bath'—a fictional wat...

item.title: Global Events Recap: July 11-17, 2026
item.description: This article compiles significant events from July 11 to 17, 2026, spanning politics, sports, and global news. Key highlights include the ongoing impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte in th...

item.title: Indian Activist Sonam Wangchuk Hospitalized After 20-Day Hunger Strike Over Education Protests
item.description: Indian activist Sonam Wangchuk, 59, was forcibly hospitalized after 20 days of hunger strike protesting India's education system. The activist, who has been fasting since June 28, demanded Education M...

item.title: Erling Haaland's Luxury Hermès Collection and Its High Cost
item.description: Erling Haaland, the Norwegian World Cup athlete, has garnered attention for his rare Hermès collection, which showcases high-end luxury items. The article highlights the cost of these exclusive pieces...

item.title: Caitlin Clark Shines in Record-Breaking WNBA Performance Amid Ongoing Controversy
item.description: Caitlin Clark, the Indiana Fever star, made history by becoming the first WNBA player to score at least 40 points and 10 assists in a game, achieving a 45-point performance against the Seattle Storm. ...

item.title: Kenyan Shilling Maintains Stability Against EAC Currencies Amid Rising Foreign Reserves
item.description: The Kenya shilling showed mixed but broadly positive performance against East African Community (EAC) currencies during the week ending July 16, 2026. While it slightly weakened against the US dollar,...

item.title: Bandits Kill 13 in Sokoto Communities Amid Security Challenges
item.description: Suspected bandits killed 13 people in fresh attacks on three communities in Sabon Birni Local Government Area of Sokoto State. The attacks, which occurred between Thursday evening and Friday morning, ...

item.title: Ontario Man Charged in Canada Day Attack on Muslim Family
item.description: A man from Ontario has been charged following a Canada Day attack on a Muslim family, according to a report by CTV Northern Ontario. The incident occurred during a public celebration, where the suspec...

item.title: Pakistan warns Iran against Houthi attacks on Saudi Arabia
item.description: Pakistan has conveyed to Iran that Houthi attacks on Saudi Arabia are considered strikes on Pakistan itself, according to a Pakistani official. The country's top civil and military leaders have emphas...

item.title: GSIS Expands Digital Services to Improve Public Administration

item.title: Sydney Sweeney and Scooter Braun's Feud with Taylor Swift: Underwear as a Symbol of the Conflict
